mnemonics: PMF = Picture My Friend (Probability Mass Function): Imagine a grid where each cell shows the probability of two friends (X and Y) meeting at that specific location. The sum of all these 'meeting probabilities' must be 1.
keyPoints: The joint PMF, P(X=x, Y=y), gives the probability that two discrete random variables take on specific values simultaneously.
Marginal distributions are found by summing the joint PMF over all possible values of the other variable (e.g., P(X=x) = Σ_all_y P(X=x, Y=y)).
Two discrete random variables are independent if and only if their joint PMF factorizes into the product of their marginal PMFs for all values: P(X=x, Y=y) = P(X=x)P(Y=y).
commonMistakes: Assuming independence without verifying the condition P(X=x, Y=y) = P(X=x)P(Y=y) holds for every possible pair (x,y).
Incorrectly calculating marginal probabilities by averaging instead of summing the joint probabilities over the other variable.
